diagnoses.name: Acute pericarditis
diagnoses.evidence_span: Initial evaluation revealed findings consistent with acute pericarditis with associated pericardial effusion

diagnoses.name: Pericardial effusion
diagnoses.evidence_span: Initial evaluation revealed findings consistent with acute pericarditis with associated pericardial effusion

diagnoses.name: Bilateral pleural effusion
diagnoses.evidence_span: Imaging revealed pleural effusion bilaterally and left basilar atelectasis on chest radiograph

diagnoses.name: Acute kidney failure
diagnoses.evidence_span: Laboratory studies demonstrated acute kidney failure with creatinine elevation to 2.1 mg/dL

diagnoses.name: Hyponatremia
diagnoses.evidence_span: significant electrolyte derangements including hypo-osmolality and hyponatremia with sodium levels as low as 128 mEq/L

diagnoses.name: Hypo-osmolality
diagnoses.evidence_span: significant electrolyte derangements including hypo-osmolality and hyponatremia with sodium levels as low as 128 mEq/L

diagnoses.name: Dehydration
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient also presented with clinical signs of dehydration despite initial fluid intake concerns

diagnoses.name: Left basilar atelectasis
diagnoses.evidence_span: Imaging revealed pleural effusion bilaterally and left basilar atelectasis on chest radiograph

diagnoses.name: Tachycardia
diagnoses.evidence_span: On admission, the patient was febrile and tachycardic

diagnoses.name: Hypotension
diagnoses.evidence_span: vital signs notable for blood pressure ranging 92-108/58-72 mmHg, reflecting other hypotension

diagnoses.name: Essential hypertension
diagnoses.evidence_span: past medical history significant for discoid lupus erythematosus, essential hypertension, and hyperlipidemia

diagnoses.name: Hyperlipidemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: past medical history significant for discoid lupus erythematosus, essential hypertension, and hyperlipidemia

diagnoses.name: Discoid lupus erythematosus
diagnoses.evidence_span: past medical history significant for discoid lupus erythematosus, essential hypertension, and hyperlipidemia

diagnoses.name: Palpitations
diagnoses.evidence_span: presented to the emergency department with acute onset chest pain, dyspnea, and palpitations

diagnoses.name: Fever
diagnoses.evidence_span: On admission, the patient was febrile and tachycardic

diagnoses.name: Dyspnea
diagnoses.evidence_span: presented to the emergency department with acute onset chest pain, dyspnea, and palpitations

procedures.name: Percutaneous drainage of pericardial cavity
procedures.evidence_span: Percutaneous drainage of the pericardial cavity was performed on hospital day 2, which yielded approximately 800 mL of serosanguineous fluid

medications.name: Normal saline
medications.evidence_span: The patient received multiple doses of intravenous normal saline

medications.name: Potassium chloride
medications.evidence_span: potassium chloride supplementation (administered in divided doses of 40, 60, and 80 mEq)

medications.name: Magnesium sulfate
medications.evidence_span: magnesium sulfate replacement (both 2 gm and 4 gm dosing)

medications.name: 0.9% sodium chloride
medications.evidence_span: IV fluid resuscitation was continued with 0.9% sodium chloride bags

medications.name: Amlodipine
medications.evidence_span: Hypertension was controlled with amlodipine and metoprolol

medications.name: Metoprolol succinate
medications.evidence_span: Hypertension was controlled with amlodipine and metoprolol (both succinate extended-release and tartrate formulations)

medications.name: Metoprolol tartrate
medications.evidence_span: Hypertension was controlled with amlodipine and metoprolol (both succinate extended-release and tartrate formulations)

medications.name: Atorvastatin
medications.evidence_span: Lipid management was continued with atorvastatin 20 mg daily

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.evidence_span: the patient received acetaminophen for pain control

medications.name: Pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine
medications.evidence_span: administration of pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine and influenza vaccine quadrivalent during hospitalization

medications.name: Influenza vaccine quadrivalent
medications.evidence_span: administration of pneumococcal 23-valent polysaccharide vaccine and influenza vaccine quadrivalent during hospitalization

chief_complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath
